AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 1 MILE WEST OF HAYFIELD ALONG HIGHWAY 30. THIS TORNADO THEN TRACKED FOR 5 MILES TO THE NORTH JUST TO THE WEST OF HIGHWAY 56 AND ENDED SOUTH OF COUNTY ROAD 6. THE MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 140 YARDS AND MAXIMUM WIND WAS 100 MPH. THE MAIN DAMAGE WAS TO TREES AND CROPS. ONE METAL SHED LOST PART OF ITS ALUMINUM ROOFING. AN OLDER BARN WAS LIFTED OFF ITS FOUNDATION AND DESTROYED.
